title: Liveness Gate + Test Ladder (adversarial-spec slice)
goals: Make it impossible to reach the gauntlet without a phase-appropriate happy-path spine test for every user story.
Force every MOCK to name the live/induced technique or be promoted to REAL-DATA.
Give the five guardrails structured, persisted, test/US-keyed output.
Provide a durable decision provenance journal (test-maturity + altitude-triage).
Define a shared TMR schema contract with fizzy, schema-first.
Classify every deliverable by verification tier; apply the this-project liveness interpretation.
Version-fence the new gates so in-flight sessions are unaffected.
non_goals: Concept-accessor facade / bindings / fail-closed lint (target-repo + its CI).
Dogfooding these changes on this session (run on current skill version).
Sessions stats dashboard (deferred, forward-only, separate trigger).
prediction-prime live-fill (Track A) and fault-injection infra (B6).
dependencies: M1 (TMR schema) is the keystone: M2, M4, M5 + the fizzy spec all key on it. Land first.
M3 (structured guardrails) is the prerequisite for M5's test-journey records.
M6 depends on M1 + M2. M7 runs alongside throughout.

applied: R1: US-8 gauntlet-only blocking, advisory on critique
carried_to_phase3: codex full spec draft as seed
rejected: codex 'cp -r' deploy step (deployed skill is a symlink)

user_stories.story: As an operator, I want a documented first-run path so I can bootstrap the liveness gate + test ladder and verify a gate locally in minutes.
criteria: First-run path documented: start post-fence session -> author one [spine]/US -> run authoring lint -> debate -> attempt gauntlet entry (pass or exit 2).
A new operator can run F' against a fixture (full-coverage->pass; missing-spine->exit 2) without reading the implementation.
Deploy reality stated: ~/.claude/skills/adversarial-spec is a symlink (edits live, no copy); no cp -r prescribed.

user_stories.story: As a spec author and fizzy, I want one agreed TMR field set + enums so the skill's emission and fizzy's validation never drift.
criteria: Schema doc enumerates every field + enum member, marked skill-owned / fizzy-owned / new.
Both repos reference the same source of truth; a field/enum mismatch is detectable, not silent.
The handshake (which repo lands the constants first) is decided.

user_stories.story: As a spec author, I want exactly one happy-path spine (TC-0) per user story with named steps, and every failure test anchored to a spine step.
criteria: One spine per US; failure tests carry spine_step_ref; an unanchored failure test is rejected at authoring.

user_stories.story: As a spec author, I want every MOCK to name the live/induced technique (or be promoted to REAL-DATA).
criteria: A MOCK without a named technique (and no genuine impossibility) is flagged; valid techniques enumerated; promotion path clear.

user_stories.story: As a spec author, I want tests tracked nl->acceptance->concrete at phase-appropriate rigor.
criteria: Each test carries a maturity; promotion rules defined; debate-time accepts nl/acceptance.

user_stories.story: As the orchestrator, I want each guardrail to run as a parallel subagent emitting structured, test/US-keyed findings.
criteria: Five guardrails run as separate subagents (never one combined prompt); each finding keyed to test/US; aggregated + persisted per round.

user_stories.story: As a spec author, I want TRACE to flag a user story with no happy-path spine as ORPHANED.
criteria: TRACE reports US-without-spine as ORPHANED and judges whether the labeled spine is the real primary path; no-test-suggestions rule holds elsewhere.

user_stories.story: As a spec author, I want TCOV to promote nl->acceptance, ingest ALL owner test files, apply strict data_strategy_mismatch, and flag missing_liveness_test.
criteria: TCOV emits PROMOTE/BLOCK; reads standalone test files; flags MOCK-without-technique; blocks missing_liveness_test on a critical-seam happy-path with no REAL/induced test.

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want a deterministic gate that blocks gauntlet entry unless every user story has a phase-appropriate-maturity happy-path spine test, with a logged override.
criteria: Gate runs in enforce_pipeline_card_gate; blocks gauntlet entry only (uncovered US -> exit 2); advisory (warn) during critique (R1).
Maturity-aware: nl/acceptance spine passes at debate->gauntlet; live-run/concrete deferred to G.
Override requires a logged >=50-char reason.

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want an append-only journal of test-maturity transitions (with drivers) to replay each user story's test journey.
criteria: Every TMR-field change appends a record (from/to/driver/phase), keyed subject_type=test; queryable as a per-US journey.

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want altitude-triage transitions journaled (created->reclassifications->close altitude_fit) to measure triage accuracy and the altitude distribution.
criteria: Node-altitude changes appended with driver; close altitude_fit in {right,too_high,too_low}; supports 'of subsystem-tagged, % right by the end' and 'distribution of altitudes identified'.

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want critical-seam/spine tests promoted to real and RUN green (at the right tier) before implementation closes.
criteria: A promotion pass writes+runs each critical-seam/spine test (code->pytest, prompt->system-validation, judgment->golden eval); un-run REAL-DATA = failing; spine/critical-seam can't be spike/exempt.

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want the new gates fenced to new specs so in-flight sessions aren't retroactively failed.
criteria: A version marker (liveness_contract_version, e.g. tmr.v1) gates the new requirements (R3); pre-fence sessions keep original rules.

user_stories.story: As a future author, I want the new vocabulary and the cross-repo scope decision recorded so terms don't collide and decisions are durable.
criteria: CONTEXT.md entries added; document-types.md row format updated; ADR records the test-ladder/TMR/liveness decision + two-spec scope split; 'happy-path spine' always qualified (no bare 'spine').

user_stories.story: As the operator, I want every deliverable classified by verification tier so this skill's own work is verified appropriately.
criteria: Each task declares a verification tier (code/prompt-doc/llm-judgment) and a consistent verification_mode; the system-validation close exercises the prompt/doc tier.

milestones.name: Guardrail rearchitecture: structured output + parallel subagents
